full_name: Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL / EMNLP / NAACL)
notes: Eight pages of content + unlimited references. ARR rolling review. Ethics statement required if involving human data or dual-use risks. Reproducibility checklist submitted separately.

sections.name: abstract
sections.heading: Abstract
sections.notes: Task, method, result, significance. Accessible to a broad NLP audience. No citations.

sections.name: introduction
sections.heading: Introduction
sections.notes: Problem motivation, challenge, your approach. Contributions as a bulleted list at the end. Include a concrete example or figure early to anchor the reader.

sections.name: related_work
sections.heading: Related Work
sections.notes: Can be placed at the end (before Conclusion) for short papers. Group by theme. Be specific about what distinguishes your work from closest prior work.

sections.name: background
sections.heading: Background
sections.notes: Task definition, datasets described here. Skip if task is standard enough to describe in Introduction.

sections.name: method
sections.heading: Method
sections.notes: Model architecture or approach. Architecture figures highly recommended. Equations numbered. Keep intuition prominent.

sections.name: experiments
sections.heading: Experiments
sections.notes: Setup, main results table, analysis. Each baseline must be cited. Error bars required. Include statistical significance where applicable.

sections.name: analysis
sections.heading: Analysis
sections.notes: Ablations, error analysis, qualitative examples. Reviewers love this section.

sections.name: conclusion
sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.notes: Summary and future directions. ACL conventions: no new claims in conclusion.

sections.name: limitations
sections.heading: Limitations
sections.notes: Mandatory since ACL 2023. Does not count toward page limit.

sections.name: ethics_statement
sections.heading: Ethics Statement
sections.notes: Required when work involves human subjects, sensitive data, or potential misuse. Does not count toward page limit.

sections.name: references
sections.heading: References
sections.notes: Unlimited pages. ACL anthology style (Author et al., Year) inline.
